sections.heading: What is Compound Interest?
sections.body: Compound interest is the addition of interest to the principal sum of a loan or deposit, or in other words, interest on interest. It is the result of reinvesting interest, rather than paying it out, so that interest in the next period is then earned on the principal sum plus previously accumulated interest.

sections.heading: Simple vs Compound Interest
sections.body: Simple interest is calculated only on the initial amount of money. Compound interest grows your money exponentially. The rule of 72 tells you how long it takes to double your money: divide 72 by your annual interest rate!

sections.heading: Start Early to Win
sections.body: Because compound interest is exponential, the key factor is time. Starting to invest at age 20 with ₹1000 per month will grow to a much larger sum by age 60 than starting at age 30 with ₹2000 per month.

quiz.question: If you earn 8% interest per year, how long will it take to double your money using the Rule of 72?
Which of these is the most important factor in compounding growth?
options: The size of the initial investment
